snippet.title: Jim Cramer interviews Nucor CEO Topalian on Mad Money.
snippet.description: Video clipped from CNBC on 2023-01-26. Must agree with Topalian's comments about the coming demand for steel through 2024 from infrastructure... but both are wrong about size...  "Cleveland-Cliffs is the Largest Flat‑Rolled Steel Company in North America."

snippet.title: Strategic Acquisition of Routejade with Dr. Raj Talluri, CEO of Enovix
snippet.description: In this episode, we talk with Enovix President & CEO, Dr. Raj Talluri about the recent acquisition of South Korean coating and battery manufacturer Routejade and the critical strategic advantages it provides as we scale up to high volume manufacturing.

localized.title: Strategic Acquisition of Routejade with Dr. Raj Talluri, CEO of Enovix
localized.description: In this episode, we talk with Enovix President & CEO, Dr. Raj Talluri about the recent acquisition of South Korean coating and battery manufacturer Routejade and the critical strategic advantages it provides as we scale up to high volume manufacturing.

snippet.title: 2023 Congressional Steel Caucus Meeting
snippet.description:
Executives from steel companies and the United Steelworkers testified before the Congressional Steel Caucus. The witnesses discussed the state of the steel industry and key steel industry policy priorities for Congress and the administration. The meeting is hosted by Steel Caucus Chairman and Vice-Chairman Reps. Rick Crawford (R-AR) and Frank Mrvan (D-IN).

Witness List: 
Lourenco Goncalves – Chairman, President and CEO, Cleveland-Cliffs Inc.
Leon Topalian –Chair, President and CEO, Nucor Corporation
Roy Houseman – Legislative Director, United Steelworkers (USW)
Richard Fruehauf – Senior Vice President, Chief Strategy & Sustainability Officer, U.S. Steel Corporation
Barbara Smith – Chairman of the Board and CEO, Commercial Metals Company
Andy Annakin – Chief Commercial Officer & EVP, Bull Moose Industries, Inc

snippet.title: Unleashing Everyday Genius with John Ferriola
snippet.description:
John Ferriola is the former CEO of Nucor, America's preeminent steelmaker. At Nucor, operating crews take responsibility for business development, capital planning, product innovation, process improvement, and cross-plant coordination. Every worker is trained in the economics of steel, and generous bonuses reward teams for boosting capital efficiency. Overhead is a fraction of the industry, while productivity and profitability are far higher.  In this episode of the New Human Movement, former CEO John Ferriola unpacks the key ingredients of Nucor's unique management approach and shares some practical lessons for leaders aiming to ignite ingenuity at scale.

▬ Episode Links ▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬

The biggest problem with capitalism? Not enough capitalists (Fortune): https://fortune.com/2020/11/21/capitalism-entrepreneurship-economic-inequality-us-bureaucracy/

Culture Eats Strategy - Nucor’s Ken Iverson on Building a Different Kind of Company: https://fs.blog/ken-iverson-nucor/
